entry.title: 🟠 Integrating S/4HANA Public Cloud APIs into CAP Made Simple
entry.content:
Integrating SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud APIs into CAP projects doesn’t have to be complex. In this session, we’ll walk through the end-to-end process of connecting a CAP application to S/4HANA Public Cloud APIs, covering authentication, service consumption, and best practices for scalability and maintainability. Whether you are a developer or an architect, you’ll gain practical insights and code samples that will help you accelerate your integration projects and unlock the full potential of SAP BTP.

SPEAKER: Sebastiano Marchesini

entry.title: 🟠 Testing SAP CAP Node.js apps with cds.test
entry.content:
This session provides a comprehensive overview of how to build and maintain automated tests for SAP CAP Node.js apps. From theoretical concepts to practical, hands-on application using the cds.test framework. Learn how to set up and execute tests for CAP services, focusing on core scenarios such as validating service APIs and handling business logic.

SPEAKER: Mauricio Lauffer

We hope you enjoyed exploring how CAP Devtoberfest session "Testing SAP CAP Node.js apps with cds.test"

Please find the Devtoberfest validation tutorial for the CAP track, Week 3

Devtoberfest validation tutorial question will be available by EOD after the 4rd CAP Devtoberfest session today.

You can find it here : https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2025-cap-week3-session-validation.html

entry.title: 🟠 CAP Tools – What's New and Hot
entry.content:
Learn what's hot and new in CAP tools, like

- IntelliJ IDEs support
- Linter checks for custom code
- CLI enhancements for debugging, deployment, repl, and more

SPEAKER: Christian Georgi

 

We hope you enjoyed exploring how CAP Devtoberfest session "CAP Tools – What's New and Hot"

Please find the Devtoberfest validation tutorial for the CAP track, Week 3

Devtoberfest validation tutorial question will be available by EOD after the 4rd CAP Devtoberfest session today.

You can find it here : https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2025-cap-week3-session-validation.html

entry.title: 🟠 Best Practices in CAP
entry.content:
In this session Daniel Hutzel - CPO and founder of CAP - will explain how to use CAP. And how to not use it... 🙂

 

We hope you enjoyed exploring how CAP Devtoberfest session "Best Practices in CAP"

Please find the Devtoberfest validation tutorial for the CAP track, Week 3

Devtoberfest validation tutorial question will be available by EOD after the 4rd CAP Devtoberfest session today.

You can find it here : https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2025-cap-week3-session-validation.html

entry.title: Re: CodeJam Request | Pune| Q1 2026- Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming M
entry.content:
Hi Ashutosh, 

Thanks for the request. We will plan for the CodeJam in Q1 2026 for Pune. 

Thanks and Regards, Ajay

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Request - Pune, India - May 2026
entry.content: @max_vandam Thanks for the request. I will discuss further for the planning in the mail.

entry.title: Re: Request to Host SAP CodeJam – Nagarro, Gurgaon
entry.content:
Hi Ashutosh,  

I have sent you the meeting invite for the planning discussion.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud at Tarento Technologies - 6th September
entry.content:
It was an eventful day at SAP CodeJam kindly hosted by Tarento Technologies India in Bengaluru. 

We thank the participants for taking a day out to spend it doing the CodeJam exercises. It was a great sight seeing the SAP Developer Community coming together, learning and networking. We had some pretty tough technical infrastructure related challenges. But it was a great team collaboration where collectively the group met the days goals in the end. Kudos to all the participants. Special thanks to @SumanthVedantam27 and @Kalyani662 for hosting the event. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/sap-cloud-application-programming-model-with-sap-hana-cloud/ec-p/13788891#M557

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
Registration is closed for this event. The first 25 participants will receive a confirmation email 2 weeks before the event and the rest of the participants will be added to a waiting list and will be considered in case of dropouts.

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Hyderabad, India.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, June 23, 2026, from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address:  Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited, Sattva Knowledge Park, Tower 1, 14th to 19th Floor Raidurg Village, Serilingampally Mandal Ranga Reddy District, Hyderabad, Telangana, 500081

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/jdstx227U4YHzABb6

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate  @ajay_soreng

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees for this event.

In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Build AI services using SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub (Gurugram, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that this is an advanced SAP CAP CodeJam and it requires participants to have basic understanding of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. 

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Location and Date

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, May 21st, 2026 from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Gurugram, India. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: Nagarro Software Private Limited, Plot 6B, Sub. Major Laxmi Chand Rd, Maruti Udyog, Sector 18, Sarhol, 122015 Gurugram, Haryana, India

Google maps location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/wwyLK7WG3Jk2Y9qw7

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 
  • Thank you Nagarro Software Private Limited for hosting this event.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About two weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance via email.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Build AI services using SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub (Gurugram, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that this is an advanced SAP CAP CodeJam and it requires participants to have basic understanding of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. 

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Location and Date

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, May 22nd, 2026 from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Gurugram, India. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: WeWork Two Horizon Centre, 5th Floor, Two Horizon Centre, 5th Floor, DLF, DLF Phase 5, Gurugram, Haryana 122009.

Google maps location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/B3LYKZoQxKMGhLby8

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 
  • Thank you MINDSET Consulting for hosting this event.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About two weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance via email.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge — Week 1: Getting familiar with the events
entry.content:
My submission for week1 . Sent the event via Business Event Generator app and received via Solace AEM broker. 

 Another one via try me feature. I choose solace because SAP Integration Suite AEM is solace under the hood.

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge — Week 2: Connecting to the broker and consuming events
entry.content:
I took a generic non sap flow to start with the Business partner generator application ==> solace AEM ==> solace message queue ==> RDP (event consumed here and sent to webhook )==> https://webhook.site/ (event finally reached  here)

I tried the last part with n8n trial but currently faced some challenges. I still try that again. 

event payload in consumer webhook.siteCloud event via Business Partner appRest delivery point

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Hyderabad, India
entry.content: Hi @pavankalyansiriki thanks for reaching out. As of now the seats are full. We will get back if there is any withdrawal/cancellation.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Hyderabad, India
entry.content: Hi @adf CodeJam event has limited seats. The email to the first 25 participants was already sent by the host.

entry.title: 🇸🇬 Build AI services using SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub (Singapore)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that this is an advanced SAP CAP CodeJam and it requires participants to have basic understanding of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. 

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Location and Date

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Wednesday, August 26, in Singapore from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

Event Address: SAP Asia Pte Ltd.30 Pasir Panjang Rd, Singapore 117440, Located in: Mapletree Business City.

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/6ZEMBPXsthyshYH2A

  • A huge thank you to Mark9 Solutions for hosting this event.
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 
  • In collaboration with Sanjog Tiwari, Customer Success Lead — AI, Data & Platform, SEA Team

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. Approximately 2 weeks prior to the event, you will receive an email to confirm your attendance, along with further instructions and details.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Build AI services using SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub (Bengaluru, India)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION IS NOW CLOSED FOR THIS EVENT - We will send confirmation emails to first 35 attendees a couple of weeks before the event and we will manage a waitlist, in case some of the RSVPs are unable to make it to the event.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that this is an advanced SAP CAP CodeJam and it requires participants to have basic understanding of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. 

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Tuesday, August 18, in Bengaluru from 9:30 AM to 4:00 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

Event Address:

Bosch Global Software Technologies
360° Business Park, Electronic City Phase I, Electronic City, Bengaluru, Doddathoguru, Karnataka 560100

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/18xB6jTQbUoyEMhp8

  •  Special thanks to Bosch Global Software Technologies  for hosting this event.
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. Approximately 2 weeks prior to the event, you will receive an email to confirm your attendance, along with further instructions and details.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🐍 July Developer Challenge — Snake & Ladder with SAP CAP Status Transition Flows
entry.content:
SAP Developer Challenge - Snake & Ladder with SAP CAP Status Transition Flows

Welcome to the July 2026 SAP Developer Challenge — a 4-week hands-on series where you build a multiplayer Snake & Ladder game as a fully functional SAP CAP service, powered by CAP Status Flows (@flow.status).

The Scenario

We will be building the backend engine for an online Snake & Ladder game. Each player's position on the board is a state — and every move, snake bite, and ladder climb is a status transition modelled using CAP's @flow.status annotations.

By the end of July, you'll have:

  • A running CAP Node.js service with CDS entities and status-flow bound actions
  • A multiplayer game engine with turn management, dice rolls, snake/ladder triggers, and CAP events
  • A SAP Fiori UI displaying the live board — participants just plug in their service URL
  • Integration with the CAP MCP server to let an AI assistant drive moves via natural language

What Makes This Interesting

CAP's @flow.status feature gives you out-of-the-box:

  • Pre-validation — a @from: [#Playing] annotation rejects any action called in the wrong state (HTTP 409), no custom code needed
  • Automatic state transitions — @TO: #Finished updates the entity after your action returns
  • History tracking — @TO: $flow.previous restores the previous state (perfect for a snake bite that temporarily blocks a player)
  • Fiori integration — buttons in the UI are automatically enabled/disabled based on the current status

Snake & Ladder maps directly onto this model: a player's turn is a state machine, and every square is a potential transition trigger.

Challenge Format

Each week a blog post is published on SAP Community with the task, hints, and code guidance. Submit your answer as a reply comment on that week's blog post.

Week Dates Title Core Topic Blog Post

16–12 JulModel the BoardCDS entities, status enum, @flow.statuslink
213–19 JulRoll & MoveBound actions, @from / @to transitionslink
320–26 JulMultiplayer & EventsTurn management, CAP events, snake/ladder logicTBD
427–31 JulFiori UI & CAP MCPBoard UI, MCP serverTBD

Prerequisites

  • Node.js 20+
  • @sap/cds-dk installed globally: npm install -g @sap/cds-dk
  • VS Code with the SAP CDS Language Support extension
  • Basic familiarity with CAP (entities, services, cds watch)

No prior knowledge of CAP status flows is required — Week 1 introduces everything from scratch.

The Board

The game uses a classic 10×10 board (squares 1–100). Key squares:

Ladders (climb up)

Bottom Top Effect

238Big early boost
831Good start
1526Small step up
2142Mid-board jump
2884The long ladder — almost wins it
3644Short gain
5167Solid advance
7191Late-game boost
7898So close to victory

Single-Headed Snakes (slide back)

Head Tail Severity

997Devastating — one square from the finish
7620Major setback
4318Mid-game punisher

Double-Headed Snakes (two biting ends, one shared tail)

These special snakes have two heads on different squares that share a single tail. Landing on either head square sends you to the same tail square.

Head 1 Head 2 Shared Tail

897250
546334
40473

The double-headed snake maps to CAP's @from: [#Head1, #Head2] — the same transition triggered by multiple entry states.

Reusable Board UI

A ready-made HTML/JS board will be provided later. Participants plug in their CAP service URL and see the live board — no frontend coding required for the challenge.

Resources

    • CAP Status Transition Flows Documentation
    • CAP Node.js Documentation
    • Initial Setup

entry.title: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
Week 1 — Model the Board

Challenge series: Snake & Ladder Developer Challenge  | Week 1 of 4 Dates: 6–12 July 2026

The Setup

Every game needs a board. Before any snake can bite or ladder can lift, you need a data model that captures the state of a game — players, positions, and crucially, whose turn it is and what can happen next.

This week we will build the CDS foundation: entities, a status enum, and your first @flow.status annotation. No game logic yet — just clean modelling and a running CAP service.

What You'll Build This Week

  • A new CAP Node.js project: snake-ladder
  • CDS entities for GameSession, Player, and BoardSquare
  • A TurnStatus enum with the game states
  • Your first @flow.status annotation
  • Seed data: a 10×10 board with all snakes and ladders pre-loaded
  • A running service at http://localhost:4004

Prerequisites

  • Node.js 20+
  • @SAP/cds-dk installed globally:
npm install -g @sap/cds-dk
  • VS Code with the SAP CDS Language Support extension. Check Initial Setup for more details how to do a local setup. 

Background: CAP Status Flows in 60 Seconds

CAP's @flow.status feature turns a regular enum field into a controlled state machine. You annotate:

  • The entity with @flow.status: FieldName to designate which field drives the state machine
  • Bound actions with @from: [#StateA] to declare which states allow that action
  • Bound actions with @TO: #StateB to declare the target state after the action runs

CAP handles the rest automatically:

  • Validates the current state before the action runs (returns HTTP 409 if wrong state)
  • Updates the status field after the action completes
  • Generates Fiori annotations to enable/disable buttons based on current state

No custom validation code. No manual status updates. The framework enforces the rules.

Step-by-Step

1. Initialise the CAP project

cds init snake-ladder --nodejs
cd snake-ladder
npm install
code .

2. Create the data model

Create db/schema.cds:

namespace snakeladder;

// ── Status enum — the heart of the state machine ──────────────────────────
type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;    // Session created, not yet started
  Playing;    // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
  Moving;     // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
  Blocked;    // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
  Finished;   // This player has reached square 100 and won
}

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data ──────────────────────
entity BoardSquares {
  key square      : Integer;        // 1–100
      snakeTo     : Integer;        // 0 if no snake on this square
      ladderTo    : Integer;        // 0 if no ladder on this square
      isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;  // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
entity GameSessions {
  key ID          : UUID;
      name        : String(100);    // e.g. "Friday Afternoon Game"
      createdAt   : Timestamp;
      startedAt   : Timestamp;
      finishedAt  : Timestamp;
      winner      : Association to Players;
}

// ── Player — one row per player per session ───────────────────────────────
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID          : UUID;
      session     : Association to GameSessions;
      name        : String(100);
      @readonly
      position    : Integer default 1;      // current square (1–100)
      @readonly
      TurnStatus  : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder   : Integer;                // 1 = goes first
      lastRoll    : Integer;               // last dice result
}

Why @flow.status on Players? Each player is an independent state machine. Their TurnStatus transitions independently — one player is Playing while others are Waiting.

3. Create the service

Create srv/game-service.cds:

using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService {

  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

    // Week 2 will implement these — declare them now so the model is complete
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @to: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @to: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @to: #Waiting                  // transitions to next player in Week 3
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @to: #Blocked                  // double-headed snake bite
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @to: $flow.previous            // restores pre-block status
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @to: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}

4. Add seed data

Create db/data/snakeladder-GameSessions.csv:

ID,name,createdAt,startedAt,finishedAt,winner_ID
a1b2c3d4-0001-0000-0000-000000000000,Demo Game,2026-07-01T09:00:00Z,,

Create db/data/snakeladder-BoardSquares.csv:

square,snakeTo,ladderTo,isDoubleHead
1,0,0,false
2,0,38,false
3,0,0,false
4,0,0,false
5,0,0,false
6,0,0,false
7,0,0,false
8,0,31,false
9,0,0,false
10,0,0,false
11,0,0,false
12,0,0,false

14,0,0,false
15,0,26,false

93,0,0,false
94,0,0,false
95,0,0,false
96,0,0,false
97,0,0,false
98,0,0,false
99,7,0,false
100,0,0,false

Double-headed snake logic: Squares 40, 47, 54, 63, 72, and 89 all have isDoubleHead: true. Pairs (40,47), (54,63), and (72,89) share a tail. Your Week 2 move handler will use this flag to apply the correct snakeTo destination.

5. Run and verify

cds watch

Open http://localhost:4004. You should see GameService with endpoints for GameSessions, Players, and BoardSquares.

Test the board data is loaded:

GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/GameService/BoardSquares?$filter=snakeTo gt 0

You should see all 9 snake-head squares returned.

How to Submit

Reply to the Week 1 blog post on SAP Community with a comment containing:

  1. Your db/schema.cds — paste as a CDS code block
  2. Your srv/game-service.cds — paste as a CDS code block
  3. A screenshot of http://localhost:4004 showing the GameService endpoints
  4. One sentence explaining your choice of TurnStatus enum values — did you add any of your own?

What's Next

In Week 2 — Roll & Move, you implement the bound actions. You'll write the rollDice handler, apply snake and ladder logic, and see CAP automatically enforce the @from / @TO transitions — returning HTTP 409 when a player tries to roll out of turn.

Resources

    • CAP Status Flows Documentation
    • CAP Node.js Documentation
    • Initial Setup 
    • cds-cli : cds add http

entry.title: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Week 2 — Roll & Move

Challenge series: Snake & Ladder Developer Challenge | Week 2 of 4 Dates: 13–19 July 2026

The Setup

You have a board. You have a player state machine. This week we will make it move.

This week you implement the bound action handlers that power the game: dice rolling, position calculation, and snake/ladder logic. CAP's @flow.status machinery will automatically enforce that moves only happen in the right state — your job is to write the business logic inside each handler.

By the end of this week you can play a full single-player game from the command line using plain OData calls.

What You'll Build This Week

  • Handler implementations for rollDice, confirmMove, blockPlayer, unblockPlayer, and winGame
  • Snake and ladder position resolution (including double-headed snake logic)
  • HTTP 409 proof: demonstrating that calling an action in the wrong state is rejected by CAP
  • An end-to-end single-player run reaching square 100

How CAP Enforces State Transitions

Before your handler code runs, CAP checks the current TurnStatus of the player entity against the @from annotation on the action. If the status doesn't match, CAP returns:

HTTP/1.1 409 Conflict
{
  "error": {
    "code": "409",
    "message": "Action 'rollDice' is not allowed in current status 'Waiting'"
  }
}

No code from you. No if (status !== 'Playing') throw .... The annotation does it.

After your handler returns (without throwing), CAP updates the status field to the @TO value. Again — no code from you.

This is the key insight of the week: your handler only contains business logic, not state management.

Step-by-Step

1. Create the service implementation file

Create srv/game-service.js:

const cds = require('@sap/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @to: #Playing      →  CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @to: #Moving       →  CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      let newPosition = player.position + roll

      // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      }

      // Persist position and last roll
      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
      // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
      // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @to: #Waiting     →  player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
      // For now: just let CAP apply @to: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @to: #Blocked     →  player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
      // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @to: #Blocked update.
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked]   →  called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @to: $flow.previous →  CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
      // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
    // @to: #Finished    →  player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
      await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        .set({
          finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          winner_ID: player.ID
        })
        .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    return super.init()
  }
}

If you notice rollDice action response looks different from how it was defined last week.

Lets adapt it as shown in srv/game-service.cds

  @from: [ #Playing ]
  @to: #Moving
  action rollDice() returns { roll: Integer; position: Integer; event: String; };

 

2. Add the domain events to your service CDS

Add these to the bottom of srv/game-service.cds:

// Domain events — emitted by handlers, consumed in Week 3
event BoardEvent {
  playerID : UUID;
  type     : String;   // 'ladder' | 'snake' | 'doubleSnake'
  from  : Integer;  // 'from' is a CDS reserved word — escape with ![]
  ![to]    : Integer;
}

event GameWon {
  playerID  : UUID;
  sessionID : UUID;
}

An error is intentionally introduced in the snippet above with the hint.  

3. Test the state machine manually

Start the server:

cds watch

First, create a player using the REST API or the Fiori Launchpad at http://localhost:4004. Then test the status flow:

Note on service path: CAP derives the OData path from the service name. GameService maps to /odata/v4/game (lowercase, stripped of the "Service" suffix). All example requests below use this path. You can confirm the mounted path in the cds watch console output: serving GameService { at: '/odata/v4/game' }.

Step 1 — Start a turn (transitions Waiting → Playing):

POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/game/Players(ID=<your-player-id>)/startTurn
Content-Type: application/json
{}

Expected response: HTTP 204 No Content (empty body). This is correct — startTurn has no return type, so CAP applies the @TO: #Playing transition silently. Verify the state changed with a GET:

GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/game/Players(ID=<your-player-id>)?$select=TurnStatus

You should see "TurnStatus": "Playing".

Step 2 — Roll the dice (transitions Playing → Moving):

POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/game/Players(ID=<your-player-id>)/rollDice
Content-Type: application/json
{}

Expected response: HTTP 200 with the dice roll, new position, and board event:

{
  "@context": "$metadata#GameService.rollDiceType",
  "roll": 4,
  "position": 38,
  "event": "ladder"
}

The event field is "normal", "ladder", "snake", or "doubleSnake". The position is the final square after any snake or ladder has been applied — so you can read the outcome directly from the action response without a follow-up GET.

Why req.reply() not return? CAP's @flow.status machinery runs an after-handler that updates the status field. If you use a plain return, that after-handler clears the response value and the client gets HTTP 204 No Content instead. Calling req.reply({...}) pins the response before the flow handler runs, so the body reaches the client.

Step 3 — Prove the 409 rejection:

Without calling confirmMove first, try to call rollDice again. CAP will return:

HTTP/1.1 409 Conflict
{
  "error": {
    "code": "409",
    "message": "Action 'rollDice' is not allowed in current status 'Moving'"
  }
}

This is the framework working exactly as designed. Screenshot this — it's your Week 2 submission evidence.

Step 4 — Confirm the move (transitions Moving → Waiting):

POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/game/Players(ID=<your-player-id>)/confirmMove
Content-Type: application/json
{}

Expected response: HTTP 204 No Content (empty body). confirmMove has no return type — the empty response is correct. Verify with a GET that TurnStatus is now "Waiting".

4. Test the double-headed snake

Manually set a player to position 88 (one roll away from square 89, a double-headed snake head):

PATCH http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/GameService/Players(ID=<your-player-id>)
Content-Type: application/json
{ "position": 88 }

Note: position is @readonly in the service — for testing, update it directly via the db entity or temporarily remove @readonly.

Then start a turn and roll — if you roll 1, you land on 89 (a double-headed snake head). Your position should move to 50 (the shared tail). The BoardEvent event should fire with type: 'doubleSnake'.

Follow up by calling blockPlayer to transition to #Blocked, then unblockPlayer to see $flow.previous restore the state.

The $flow.previous Moment

When you call unblockPlayer and see the status return to its previous value automatically — that's CAP's state history at work. No code, no stored "previousStatus" field. The framework tracks it.

Note: $flow.previous restores whatever status the entity held before the transition to #Blocked. In this flow, blockPlayer is called from #Moving, so unblockPlayer restores #Moving — not #Waiting. The game loop then calls confirmMove to finish the turn. If you call blockPlayer from a different status in future extensions, $flow.previous will restore that one instead.

Stretch Goal

Add a skipTurn action that can be called when a player is #Blocked, as an alternative to unblockPlayer, that uses @TO: $flow.previous as well but also increments a turnsBlocked counter on the player:

@from: [ #Blocked ]
@to: $flow.previous
action skipTurn();
this.on('skipTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
  const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
  await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ turnsBlocked: (player.turnsBlocked || 0) + 1 })
    .where({ ID: player.ID })
})

How to Submit

Reply to the Week 2 blog post on SAP Community with a comment containing:

  1. Your srv/game-service.js handler implementations (paste as a JS code block)
  2. A screenshot showing the HTTP 409 response when calling rollDice in the wrong state
  3. A screenshot or OData response showing your player's position after landing on a snake or ladder
  4. One sentence: what happens if you call winGame on a player whose position is not 100? How does your implementation handle it?

What's Next

In Week 3 — Multiplayer & Events, you add a second player, implement turn rotation, and wire up a CAP event subscriber that automatically advances the game state when a move is confirmed.
